items.title: Two Pointer Technique
items.content: Use two pointers moving towards each other or same direction. Classic: Two Sum (sorted), Remove duplicates, Container with most water. Time: O(n), Space: O(1).

items.title: Sliding Window Pattern
items.content: Maintain window of elements. Expand/shrink based on condition. Use for: Substring problems, max/min subarray. Fixed vs dynamic window. Time: O(n).

items.title: Kadane's Algorithm
items.content: Find maximum subarray sum. Track current sum, reset if negative. Classic DP problem with O(n) solution. Key: Local max vs global max.

items.title: Dutch National Flag
items.content: Three-way partitioning. Sort array of 0s, 1s, 2s in O(n) time. Use 3 pointers: low, mid, high. Applications: 3-way quicksort, color sorting.

items.title: Hash Table Fundamentals
items.content: Key-value storage. Average O(1) insert/lookup. Handle collisions: Chaining or open addressing. Load factor affects performance. Rehashing for scaling.

items.title: Hash Set Applications
items.content: Detect duplicates O(n). Check existence. Two Sum using HashMap. Frequency counting. Anagram grouping. Trade space for time.

items.title: Min/Max Heap Operations
items.content: Complete binary tree. Parent ≤ children (min-heap). Insert: O(log n), Extract-min: O(log n), Peek: O(1). Heapify: O(n). Applications: Priority queue.

items.title: Top K Elements Pattern
items.content: Maintain K-size heap. For Kth largest: use min-heap of size K. For Kth smallest: use max-heap. Time: O(n log k). Space: O(k).

items.title: KMP Pattern Matching
items.content: Efficient string search. Build LPS (Longest Prefix Suffix) array. Time: O(m+n) vs naive O(m*n). Useful for: Pattern matching, substring search.

items.title: Trie (Prefix Tree)
items.content: Tree for string storage/search. Fast prefix matching O(m). Use for: Autocomplete, spell check, IP routing. Space trade-off for speed.

items.title: Binary Tree Traversals
items.content: Inorder (L-Root-R), Preorder (Root-L-R), Postorder (L-R-Root). Recursive vs Iterative (using stack). Level-order uses queue. Time: O(n).

items.title: LCA (Lowest Common Ancestor)
items.content: Find common ancestor of two nodes. BST: Compare values. Binary tree: Recursive search both sides. Applications: Organization charts, file systems.

items.title: Balanced BST Operations
items.content: AVL/Red-Black trees maintain O(log n) height. Rotations keep balance. Insert/Delete/Search: O(log n). Know when to use vs HashMap.

items.title: DFS vs BFS
items.content: DFS: Stack/Recursion, explores depth. Use: Cycle detection, topological sort. BFS: Queue, explores breadth. Use: Shortest path, level-order.

items.title: Dijkstra's Algorithm
items.content: Shortest path from source to all nodes. Use min-heap. Time: O((V+E)logV). Doesn't work with negative weights. Real-world: GPS, network routing.

items.title: Topological Sort
items.content: Linear ordering of directed acyclic graph (DAG). Use DFS or Kahn's algorithm. Applications: Task scheduling, build systems, course prerequisites.

items.title: Union Find (Disjoint Set)
items.content: Track connected components. Union: Connect sets. Find: Get root. Path compression + union by rank: Nearly O(1). Use: Detect cycles, Kruskal's MST.

items.title: Backtracking Pattern
items.content: Explore all possibilities, backtrack on dead ends. Template: Choose → Explore → Unchoose. Use for: Permutations, combinations, N-Queens, Sudoku.

items.title: Pruning Optimization
items.content: Skip invalid branches early. Check constraints before recursion. Memoization for overlapping subproblems. Reduces exponential to manageable time.

items.title: Common Bit Operations
items.content: Set bit: x | (1<<i). Clear: x & ~(1<<i). Toggle: x ^ (1<<i). Check: (x & (1<<i)) != 0. Count set bits: Brian Kernighan's algorithm.

items.title: XOR Properties
items.content: a ^ a = 0, a ^ 0 = a. Commutative & Associative. Use: Find single number, swap without temp, detect different bits. Parity checking.

items.title: DP Pattern Recognition
items.content: Look for: Optimal substructure, overlapping subproblems. Ask: Can I break into smaller problems? Am I solving same thing multiple times? Memoize!

items.title: Knapsack Variations
items.content: 0/1 Knapsack: Can't repeat items. Unbounded: Infinite items. Fractional: Greedy works. DP table: dp[i][w] = max value with i items, w capacity.

items.title: Longest Common Subsequence
items.content: Find longest subsequence in both strings. 2D DP: dp[i][j] = LCS of first i chars of s1, j chars of s2. Time: O(m*n). Base for diff, edit distance.

items.title: Coin Change Variations
items.content: Min coins for amount: dp[i] = min(dp[i-coin] + 1). Count ways: dp[i] += dp[i-coin]. Order matters vs not. Unbounded knapsack variant.

items.title: QuickSort & Partitioning
items.content: Divide-conquer. Pick pivot, partition around it. Average O(n log n), worst O(n²). In-place. Use partition logic for QuickSelect (Kth element).

items.title: MergeSort Stability
items.content: Stable sort: Preserves relative order. Guaranteed O(n log n). Not in-place: O(n) space. Use for: External sorting, linked lists, when stability needed.

items.title: Time Complexity Classes
items.content: O(1) < O(log n) < O(n) < O(n log n) < O(n²) < O(2ⁿ) < O(n!). Know when each is acceptable. n=10⁶: O(n log n) OK, O(n²) timeout.

items.title: Space Complexity Tradeoffs
items.content: Recursive stack space: O(depth). Memoization: Trade space for time. In-place algorithms save space. Hash tables: O(n) space for O(1) lookup.

items.title: Singleton Pattern
items.content: One instance globally. Thread-safe: Double-checked locking or enum. Use: Config, Logger, DB connection pool. Caution: Global state, testing issues.

items.title: Factory Pattern
items.content: Create objects without exposing creation logic. Abstract Factory: Family of related objects. Use: Different DB drivers, UI components. Decouples client from concrete classes.

items.title: Observer Pattern
items.content: One-to-many dependency. Subject notifies observers of state changes. Use: Event systems, MVC, pub-sub. Example: Newsletter subscriptions, stock price updates.

items.title: Strategy Pattern
items.content: Encapsulate algorithms, make them interchangeable. Define family of algorithms. Use: Payment methods, sorting algorithms, compression. Avoids if-else chains.

items.title: Dependency Inversion
items.content: Depend on abstractions, not concretions. High-level modules shouldn't depend on low-level. Use interfaces. Enables testing, flexibility, decoupling.

items.title: Interface Segregation
items.content: Many specific interfaces > one general. Clients shouldn't depend on unused methods. Split fat interfaces. Prevents unnecessary coupling.

items.title: Composition vs Inheritance
items.content: Favor composition over inheritance. Inheritance: IS-A. Composition: HAS-A. Composition more flexible, avoids deep hierarchies. Use interfaces for contracts.

items.title: Encapsulation Best Practices
items.content: Hide internal state. Expose behavior, not data. Use private fields, public methods. Validate in setters. Immutability when possible. Prevents invariant violations.

items.title: Load Balancer
items.content: Distribute traffic across servers. L4 (Transport) vs L7 (Application). Algorithms: Round-robin, least connections, IP hash. Handle health checks, SSL termination.

items.title: API Gateway
items.content: Single entry point for clients. Handles: Routing, auth, rate limiting, logging, aggregation. Prevents coupling clients to microservices. Can become bottleneck.

items.title: Message Queue
items.content: Async communication. Producers/Consumers decoupled. Use: RabbitMQ, Kafka, SQS. Provides: Buffering, retry, ordering. Pattern: Task queues, pub-sub, event streaming.

items.title: Cache Layer
items.content: Reduce DB load, improve latency. Types: Client, CDN, App, DB. Redis/Memcached. Patterns: Cache-aside, write-through. Handle: Invalidation, expiration, consistency.

items.title: SQL Database Design
items.content: ACID guarantees. Use for: Transactions, complex queries, relationships. Scaling: Read replicas, sharding, vertical. Indexes trade write speed for read speed.

items.title: NoSQL Database Types
items.content: Key-Value: Redis, DynamoDB. Document: MongoDB. Column: Cassandra. Graph: Neo4j. Choose based on: Access patterns, scalability needs, consistency requirements.

items.title: Database Replication
items.content: Master-Slave: Writes to master, reads from slaves. Master-Master: Both accept writes, complex conflict resolution. Async vs sync. Handle: Lag, failover.

items.title: ACID Properties
items.content: Atomicity: All or nothing. Consistency: Valid state always. Isolation: Concurrent transactions don't interfere. Durability: Committed data persists.

items.title: Normalization Levels
items.content: 1NF: Atomic values. 2NF: No partial dependencies. 3NF: No transitive dependencies. BCNF: Every determinant is key. Denormalize for read performance.

items.title: Race Conditions
items.content: Multiple threads access shared data, at least one writes. Result depends on timing. Fix: Locks, mutexes, atomic operations, immutability.

items.title: Deadlock Prevention
items.content: Conditions: Mutual exclusion, hold & wait, no preemption, circular wait. Prevent: Lock ordering, timeouts, deadlock detection. Avoid nested locks.

items.title: Thread Pool Pattern
items.content: Reuse threads for multiple tasks. Avoid thread creation overhead. Bounded queue for tasks. Configure: Pool size, queue size, rejection policy.

items.title: Rate Limiting Algorithms
items.content: Token Bucket: Tokens refill at rate. Leaky Bucket: Fixed outflow rate. Fixed/Sliding Window. Use Redis for distributed. Prevents abuse, ensures fairness.

items.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
items.content: Distribute static content globally. Edge locations close to users. Caches: Images, CSS, JS, videos. Reduces latency, bandwidth. Invalidation strategies important.

items.title: Database Indexing Strategy
items.content: B-tree for range queries. Hash for exact match. Composite for multiple columns. Too many slows writes. Monitor query plans. Index selectivity matters.

items.title: Retry with Exponential Backoff
items.content: Retry failed operations with increasing delays. Add jitter to prevent thundering herd. Set max retries. Use for: Network calls, queue processing. Handle idempotency.

items.title: Health Checks & Monitoring
items.content: Liveness: Is service running? Readiness: Can it handle traffic? Deep vs shallow checks. Metrics: Latency, throughput, errors. Alerts: SLI, SLO, SLA based.

items.title: Graceful Degradation
items.content: Reduce functionality instead of complete failure. Example: Show cached data if DB down. Circuit breaker prevents cascade. Prioritize core features. Communicate to users.

items.title: Idempotency
items.content: Same request multiple times = same result. Critical for retries. Use unique request IDs. POST not idempotent, PUT/DELETE are. Prevent duplicate payments, orders.

items.title: Connection Pooling
items.content: Reuse DB connections. Avoid connection overhead. Configure: Min/max pool size, connection timeout, idle timeout. Monitor pool saturation.

items.title: Lazy Loading
items.content: Load data only when needed. Reduces initial load time. Use for: Large lists, images, modules. Watch for: N+1 query problem, over-fetching.

items.title: Exception Handling Best Practices
items.content: Catch specific exceptions, not generic. Log context, don't swallow errors. Fail fast for programming errors. Retry for transient failures. Clean up resources.

items.title: Circuit Breaker States
items.content: Closed: Normal operation. Open: Fail fast, don't call service. Half-Open: Test if service recovered. Thresholds trigger state changes.

items.title: Service Discovery
items.content: Client-side: Client queries registry (Consul, Eureka). Server-side: Load balancer handles. DNS-based. Handle: Registration, health checks, failover.

items.title: API Versioning
items.content: URI: /v1/users. Header: Accept: application/vnd.api+json;version=1. Semantic versioning. Deprecation policy. Support multiple versions temporarily. Communicate changes.

items.title: Distributed Tracing
items.content: Track requests across services. Correlation IDs. Use: Jaeger, Zipkin. Identify bottlenecks, debug issues. Sampling for production. Understand service dependencies.

items.title: Sync vs Async Communication
items.content: Sync: REST, gRPC. Tight coupling, simple. Async: Message queues, events. Loose coupling, complex. Choose based on: Consistency needs, latency tolerance.

items.title: Database per Service
items.content: Each microservice owns its data. No shared DB. Benefits: Independence, scalability. Challenges: Transactions, joins. Use: Saga pattern, event sourcing, CQRS.

items.title: System Design Framework
items.content: 1. Requirements (functional, non-functional). 2. Capacity estimates. 3. High-level design. 4. Deep dive components. 5. Bottlenecks & trade-offs. Ask clarifying questions!

items.title: Coding Problem Strategy
items.content: 1. Clarify requirements. 2. Examples/edge cases. 3. Brute force first. 4. Optimize (time/space). 5. Code clean solution. 6. Test with examples. 7. Analyze complexity.

items.title: LLD Framework
items.content: 1. Understand requirements. 2. Identify core entities. 3. Define relationships. 4. Apply design patterns. 5. SOLID principles. 6. Draw class diagram. 7. Discuss trade-offs.

items.title: STAR Method
items.content: Situation: Set context. Task: What needed to be done. Action: What YOU did. Result: Outcome with metrics. Use for: Leadership, conflict, failure, success stories.

items.title: Questions for Team Assessment
items.content: Tech: Stack, architecture challenges, tech debt. Team: Size, structure, collaboration. Growth: Learning opportunities, career path. Process: Development, deployment, on-call.

items.title: RESTful Principles
items.content: Stateless, resource-based URLs. HTTP methods: GET (read), POST (create), PUT (update), DELETE. Status codes: 2xx success, 4xx client error, 5xx server error.

items.title: Pagination Strategies
items.content: Offset-based: page/limit (simple, jump to page). Cursor-based: Stable for real-time data, no skipped records. Keyset: Use last seen ID. Response: data + metadata.

items.title: Authentication vs Authorization
items.content: Authentication: Who are you? (Login, tokens). Authorization: What can you do? (Permissions, roles). JWT: Self-contained tokens. OAuth2: Delegated access.

items.title: Common Vulnerabilities
items.content: SQL Injection: Use parameterized queries. XSS: Sanitize input, escape output. CSRF: Use tokens. SSRF: Validate URLs. Principle of least privilege.

items.title: Test Pyramid
items.content: Many unit tests (fast, isolated). Fewer integration tests (modules together). Few E2E tests (slow, brittle). Mock external dependencies. Aim for 80% coverage.

items.title: TDD Red-Green-Refactor
items.content: Red: Write failing test. Green: Make it pass (minimal code). Refactor: Clean up while tests pass. Benefits: Better design, confidence to refactor.

items.title: Handling Disagreements
items.content: Listen first, understand their perspective. Present data/reasoning, not just opinion. Find common ground. Escalate if needed. Focus on outcome, not being right.

items.title: Effective Mentoring
items.content: Ask questions, don't just give answers. Share context, explain 'why'. Pair programming for hands-on learning. Regular 1-on-1s. Celebrate growth, give constructive feedback.

items.title: Managing Technical Priorities
items.content: Urgent vs Important matrix. Say no to low-impact work. Delegate when possible. Time-box research. Balance new features vs tech debt. Communicate trade-offs.
